title: Merge duplicate is_cycle loops and extract SCC module from lib.rs
analysis:
The original detect_recursive_structures() in lib.rs has two separate loops over st.sccs that both compute is_cycle identically: (1) tags in_cycle[v] and emits warning diagnostics, (2) builds cyclic_sccs Vec<HashSet<String>>. These should be merged into a single pass. Additionally, ~240 lines of Tarjan SCC code (detect_recursive_structures, TarjanState, tarjan_scc_visit, reconstruct_scc_cycle, find_cycle_back_to) should be extracted into a new scc.rs module, following the precedent of module_dag.rs which is already extracted. This is a pure refactor — no new behavior. Existing integration tests in recursive_detection_tests.rs (12 tests) and termination_check_tests.rs (12 tests) exercise the SCC detection and termination checking through the public compile() function. The implementation commit (14fd7663) is complete on the task branch. Steps 1-11 completed the SCC refactor and verified the merge to main.

POST-REVIEW UPDATE 1 (steps 12-16): Review identified two blocking issues. (1) design_coherence_invariant_handling: Task 351 (merged to main at d5b1123a) converted expect("compiler bug") to ? (silent None propagation) on Extrude/Revolve arg lookups in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:3237-3278. This violates the project convention that logic errors must be noisy (feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md). Missing args and non-f64 results are compiler invariants — the compiler must always emit these, so absence is a bug. The fix restores expect()/panic!() on invariant checks while keeping the NaN/Inf filter added by ba327379 (legitimate runtime check). (2) merge_ff_failed: Main advanced after our initial merge; the SCC refactor commit 2bbe33c6 IS already an ancestor of main, but the unmerged commits needed the invariant-handling fix.

POST-REVIEW UPDATE 2 (steps 17-20): Step-16 merge was superseded — main advanced again (~120 commits since merge base 2bbe33c6, HEAD now at 7d6b54c6). The SCC refactor remains in main. The invariant-fix commits (b662570e test, 55212594 impl) need rebase onto current main. Conflicts expected in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs because: (a) main independently changed the revolve missing-arg test from _panics to _returns_none (ade9feaa), (b) main added NaN axis test (task-364: c7dd0ffe), (c) both sides modified eval_arg_f64 closure differently (main added .filter(), task replaced ? with panic). Resolution: keep task's invariant assertions (expect/panic), keep task's #[should_panic] tests, keep main's NaN test, keep .filter(|v| v.is_finite()) in the restructured closure.

POST-REVIEW UPDATE 3 (steps 21-29): Review round 3 found three blocking issues. (1) merge_ff_failed: Main advanced again — task/409 has 2 commits (9efd1ed3, a032f1d3) not in main; main gained task-351 rebase and status-update commits. Rebase + merge needed. (2) untested_failure_mode: Extrude distance is not filtered for NaN/Inf — the evaluated Value is passed directly into GeometryOp::Extrude without finiteness validation, while Revolve has .filter(|v| v.is_finite()). The asymmetry means NaN/Inf extrude distances silently reach the OCCT kernel. Fix: add `distance.as_f64().filter(|v| v.is_finite())?;` after the expect, plus tests for NaN and Inf cases. (3) silent_failure_on_invariant_violation: reconstruct_scc_cycle in scc.rs:178-182 silently falls back to listing all SCC members when find_cycle_back_to returns None — a 'should not happen' path that violates feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md. Fix: add debug_assert!(false, ...) before the fallback so debug/test builds panic on this invariant violation.

steps.description: Verify all 24 existing recursive_detection_tests and termination_check_tests pass on main before refactoring (baseline confirmation that no test regressions exist pre-change)

Merge the two identical loops over st.sccs in detect_recursive_structures(): combine in_cycle tagging + diagnostic emission + cyclic_sccs collection into a single pass with one is_cycle computation per SCC
Verify all recursive_detection_tests and termination_check_tests still pass after loop merge (behavior-preserving refactor)

Create crates/reify-compiler/src/scc.rs: move detect_recursive_structures (as pub(crate)), TarjanState, tarjan_scc_visit, reconstruct_scc_cycle, find_cycle_back_to from lib.rs into the new module. Add 'mod scc;' to lib.rs and update the call site to scc::detect_recursive_structures
Verify all recursive_detection_tests, termination_check_tests, and full compiler test suite pass after module extraction (ensures no broken imports or visibility issues)
Rebase onto current main and retry merge (previous merge failed because main advanced during the merge window)
Rebase task/409 onto current main (d5b1123a) to incorporate task/351 merge that advanced main after step-6. No conflicts expected since main's new commits don't touch crates/reify-compiler/.
Verify all recursive_detection_tests, termination_check_tests, and full compiler test suite still pass after rebase onto updated main (confirms no regressions from task/351 interaction)
Fast-forward merge task/409 into main. Verify merge succeeded and main HEAD contains the refactor commit.

Verify merge is already present in main: confirm 2bbe33c6 is an ancestor of main via git merge-base --is-ancestor, and confirm scc.rs exists in main's tree. The merge_ff_failed review feedback was a false positive — main advanced after our merge landed but our commit is reachable from current main HEAD (114a1ac0).
Run recursive_detection_tests (12 tests) and termination_check_tests (12 tests) against current main to confirm no regressions from the ~30 commits that landed after our merge.

Reset task/409 to current main HEAD (all SCC refactor work from steps 1-11 is already merged). Drop unmerged commits 325e35f7 (status update) and 87bb12fd (stale test adaptation) — both will be superseded by the invariant-handling fix. This gives a clean baseline with main's current eval code including the NaN/Inf filter from ba327379.

Write failing tests for compiler invariant assertions in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs: (1) Restore #[should_panic(expected = "compiler bug")] on compile_geometry_op_revolve_missing_arg test — rename back to _panics, remove assert!(result.is_none()) and call compile_geometry_op directly (will fail because code returns None instead of panicking). (2) Add new #[test] #[should_panic(expected = "compiler bug")] fn compile_geometry_op_extrude_missing_distance_panics() that constructs an Extrude Sweep with empty args vec and calls compile_geometry_op (will fail because code returns None instead of panicking).

Restore invariant assertions in compile_geometry_op (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:3236-3275): (1) Extrude distance (line ~3239): replace trailing `?` with `.expect("Extrude distance arg missing — compiler bug")`. (2) Revolve eval_arg_f64 closure (lines ~3250-3256): replace `find(|(n, _)| n == name)?` with `.find(...).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("Revolve Sweep '{}' arg missing — compiler bug", name))`, then replace the implicit `as_f64()` in the `?` chain with `.as_f64().unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("Revolve '{}' arg must evaluate to f64 — compiler bug", name))`, and wrap the result: `Some(val).filter(|v| v.is_finite())` to keep the NaN/Inf filter. Closure return type stays `-> Option<f64>` since NaN/Inf legitimately returns None.

Verify all eval tests pass: (1) #[should_panic] tests for Revolve missing-arg and Extrude missing-distance both pass (invariant violations now panic). (2) compile_geometry_op_revolve_nan_axis_returns_none still returns None (NaN is runtime, not invariant). (3) compile_geometry_op_revolve_zero_axis_returns_none still returns None. (4) Full `cargo test --package reify-eval` passes. (5) Full `cargo test --package reify-compiler` passes (no regressions to SCC refactor).

Merge task/409 into main via fast-forward (or rebase if main advanced). Verify merge succeeded with git merge-base --is-ancestor and that both scc.rs and the restored invariant assertions are present in main's tree.

Rebase invariant-fix commits (b662570e, 55212594) onto current main HEAD (7d6b54c6). Main advanced ~120 commits since merge base 2bbe33c6. Conflicts expected in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs: (a) eval_arg_f64 closure — main added .filter(|v| v.is_finite()) after .as_f64()?, task replaced entire ? chain with unwrap_or_else(panic) + Some(val).filter(); resolve by keeping task's restructured closure with both panic assertions and filter. (b) revolve missing-arg test — main renamed _panics→_returns_none (ade9feaa), task keeps _panics with #[should_panic]; resolve by keeping task's _panics version. (c) main added compile_geometry_op_revolve_nan_axis_returns_none test (task-364); resolve by keeping it alongside task's new extrude test. After rebase, task branch should have exactly 2 commits on top of main.

Verify all tests pass after rebase: (1) cargo test --package reify-eval — confirms #[should_panic] tests pass, NaN/zero-axis tests pass, no new failures from main's changes. (2) cargo test --package reify-compiler — confirms SCC refactor still works with latest main. (3) Spot-check that compile_geometry_op_revolve_nan_axis_returns_none (main's test) coexists with compile_geometry_op_revolve_missing_arg_panics (task's test).

Fast-forward merge task/409 into main. If main advanced again during rebase, re-rebase and retry (up to 2 retries). Verify with git merge-base --is-ancestor that the new HEAD of task/409 is an ancestor of main after merge.

Post-merge verification on main: (1) Confirm scc.rs exists in main's tree. (2) Confirm invariant assertions (expect/panic) are present in compile_geometry_op. (3) Confirm .filter(|v| v.is_finite()) is preserved. (4) Run cargo test --package reify-eval and cargo test --package reify-compiler one final time from main HEAD.

Write two failing tests in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs for the Extrude NaN/Inf gap: (1) `compile_geometry_op_extrude_nan_distance_returns_none` — constructs an Extrude Sweep with a valid profile and `literal_f64(f64::NAN)` as the distance arg, asserts compile_geometry_op returns None. Will FAIL because current code passes NaN through to GeometryOp::Extrude without checking finiteness. (2) `compile_geometry_op_extrude_inf_distance_returns_none` — same but with `literal_f64(f64::INFINITY)` as distance. Will also FAIL for the same reason. These mirror the existing `compile_geometry_op_revolve_nan_axis_returns_none` test pattern.

Add finiteness check to the Extrude arm in compile_geometry_op (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:3226-3234): after the `.expect("compiler bug")` that produces the `distance: Value`, add a guard `distance.as_f64().filter(|v| v.is_finite())?;` before constructing the GeometryOp::Extrude. This returns None when the distance is NaN, Inf, or non-numeric (same pattern as Revolve's eval_arg_f64 closure). The full Value is still passed to the struct when finite. Verify both new tests now pass.

Write a unit test in crates/reify-compiler/src/scc.rs inside a new `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block: `#[test] #[cfg(debug_assertions)] #[should_panic(expected = "Tarjan algorithm invariant violated")]` fn `reconstruct_scc_cycle_panics_on_invalid_scc`. Construct 2 minimal TopologyTemplate values (name='A', name='B', all other fields default/empty) and an adjacency list `vec![vec![1], vec![]]` where node 0→1 but 1 has no edges — so find_cycle_back_to(0, {0,1}, adj) returns None. Call `reconstruct_scc_cycle(&[0, 1], &adjacency, &templates)`. This test will FAIL because the current else branch silently falls back instead of asserting.

Replace the silent fallback in reconstruct_scc_cycle (crates/reify-compiler/src/scc.rs:178-182): change the else branch from a plain fallback to `debug_assert!(false, "find_cycle_back_to returned None for valid SCC — Tarjan algorithm invariant violated");` followed by the existing fallback code. This makes the invariant violation noisy in debug/test builds (panic via debug_assert) while keeping the degraded-but-safe path for release builds. Add a comment explaining the intentional lossy fallback in release mode.

Verify all tests pass after both fixes: (1) `cargo test --package reify-eval` — confirms both new extrude NaN/Inf tests pass alongside existing revolve NaN/zero-axis tests, #[should_panic] invariant tests, and all other eval tests. (2) `cargo test --package reify-compiler` — confirms new SCC debug_assert test passes (in debug build) alongside all existing recursive_detection_tests and termination_check_tests.

Rebase task/409 onto current main HEAD. Task branch currently has 2 old commits (9efd1ed3, a032f1d3) plus new commits from steps 21-24 on top of merge-base 8348674c. Main has task-351 rebase and status commits. Conflicts expected in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs (Extrude/Revolve arm code differs between branches — main uses `?` for missing args, task uses expect/panic). Resolution: keep all of task's changes (invariant assertions + new NaN/Inf guard + new tests). Conflicts also possible in scc.rs if main touched it — keep task's version with debug_assert. After rebase, verify commit count and content match expectations.

Post-rebase verification: (1) `cargo test --package reify-eval` — all eval tests pass on rebased branch. (2) `cargo test --package reify-compiler` — all compiler tests pass on rebased branch. (3) Spot-check: extrude NaN/Inf tests exist, scc.rs has debug_assert, invariant panics are present.

Fast-forward merge task/409 into main. If main advanced during rebase, re-rebase and retry (up to 2 retries). Verify with `git merge-base --is-ancestor` that task/409 HEAD is an ancestor of main after merge.

Post-merge verification on main: (1) Confirm scc.rs has debug_assert in reconstruct_scc_cycle. (2) Confirm Extrude arm has `.as_f64().filter(|v| v.is_finite())?` guard. (3) Confirm invariant assertions (expect/panic) remain. (4) Run `cargo test --package reify-eval` and `cargo test --package reify-compiler` from main HEAD — all tests green.

design_decisions.decision: Use pub(crate) visibility for detect_recursive_structures in scc.rs
design_decisions.rationale: The function is called from lib.rs compile() but is not part of the public API. pub(crate) matches the module-internal usage pattern without leaking implementation details.

design_decisions.decision: Keep TarjanState, tarjan_scc_visit, reconstruct_scc_cycle, find_cycle_back_to as private functions within scc.rs
design_decisions.rationale: These are implementation details of the SCC algorithm. Only detect_recursive_structures is the public entry point for the module. This mirrors how module_dag.rs exposes ModuleDag/ModuleResolver but keeps helpers private.

design_decisions.decision: No new tests needed — rely on existing integration tests
design_decisions.rationale: This is a pure structural refactor (loop merge + module extraction) with zero behavior change. The 12 recursive_detection_tests and 12 termination_check_tests exercise all SCC-related behavior through the public compile() API, which is the correct abstraction boundary for testing.

design_decisions.decision: Both changes (loop merge + extraction) combined in a single commit
design_decisions.rationale: The two changes are tightly coupled (both touch the same ~250-line block) and neither is useful without the other. A single atomic commit avoids an intermediate state where the loop is merged but not extracted.

design_decisions.decision: No rebase/re-merge needed for merge_ff_failed feedback
design_decisions.rationale: Investigation confirmed commit 2bbe33c6 is already an ancestor of main (git merge-base --is-ancestor exits 0). The review feedback was a false positive — main advanced with other task merges after our merge landed. Steps 10-11 verify this rather than re-merging.

design_decisions.decision: Restore expect()/panic!() for compiler-invariant arg lookups, keep NaN/Inf filter as Option
design_decisions.rationale: Missing args (find returns None) and non-f64 evaluation results (as_f64 returns None) are compiler invariants — the compiler must always emit these args with the correct types, so their absence is a bug that must be loud per feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md. NaN/Inf from user expressions is a legitimate runtime edge case (not a compiler bug), so the .filter(|v| v.is_finite()) correctly returns None and lets the caller emit a diagnostic. This separation preserves both the loud-invariant contract and the graceful NaN handling added by ba327379.

design_decisions.decision: Reset task/409 to main rather than rebase stale commits
design_decisions.rationale: The two unmerged commits (status update + test adaptation) are both stale — the status update is ephemeral and the test adaptation endorsed the wrong pattern. Resetting to main and applying fixes fresh avoids rebase conflicts with ade9feaa (which made the same test change on main independently).

design_decisions.decision: Rebase 2 commits rather than cherry-pick or squash for third merge attempt
design_decisions.rationale: The task branch has exactly 2 meaningful commits (b662570e test, 55212594 impl) on top of the already-merged SCC refactor. Rebasing preserves the TDD commit history (test-first, then impl) and produces a clean fast-forward merge. Cherry-pick would duplicate commits; squash would lose the test-impl separation.

design_decisions.decision: Extrude NaN/Inf guard checks Value.as_f64() then filters, preserving the full Value in the struct
design_decisions.rationale: GeometryOp::Extrude stores distance as a Value (not f64) to preserve unit/dimension info. The finiteness check uses .as_f64().filter(|v| v.is_finite())? as a gate — if the underlying number is NaN/Inf, the op is skipped (returns None). If finite, the original Value (with Scalar type, dimension, etc.) is passed through unchanged. This mirrors Revolve's eval_arg_f64 pattern but operates on Value instead of f64.

design_decisions.decision: Use debug_assert!(false, ...) + fallback for SCC invariant, not unreachable!()
design_decisions.rationale: unreachable!() would crash release builds if the invariant is ever violated (e.g., due to a subtle bug in the SCC adjacency construction). debug_assert!(false, ...) + fallback gives the best of both worlds: noisy failure in debug/test builds (satisfying feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md), graceful degradation in release builds (the fallback message is slightly less precise but still useful). The fallback path is documented as intentionally lossy.

design_decisions.decision: Unit test for SCC invariant uses #[cfg(debug_assertions)] gate
design_decisions.rationale: The debug_assert only fires in debug builds. The #[should_panic] test must be gated with #[cfg(debug_assertions)] so it doesn't fail in release-mode test runs (where debug_assert is a no-op). This is standard practice for testing debug_assert behavior.

reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: declare private module in lib.rs with 'mod scc;', expose entry point as pub(crate), keep implementation details private

reuse.what: Existing integration tests for SCC detection
reuse.how: 12 tests covering self-reference, indirect cycles (2-node, 3-node), non-cyclic graphs (linear chains, diamonds), multiple independent cycles, and diagnostic messages — all exercise detect_recursive_structures through compile()

reuse.what: Existing integration tests for termination checking
reuse.how: 12 tests covering termination condition validation that depend on correct SCC detection output (cyclic_sccs) — validates the merged loop produces correct cyclic_sccs downstream

reuse.what: Existing NaN/Inf and zero-axis test patterns
reuse.how: These tests verify that NaN/Inf values and zero-length axes correctly return None — they confirm the NaN/Inf filter is preserved after restoring invariant panics on arg lookups. The new Extrude NaN/Inf tests follow this exact pattern.

reuse.what: Original expect() message patterns from pre-task-351 code
reuse.how: Restore the same message format: 'Extrude Sweep args must contain distance key — compiler bug', 'Revolve Sweep X arg missing — compiler bug', 'Revolve X arg must evaluate to f64 — compiler bug'

reuse.what: literal_f64 test helper for NaN/Inf test construction
reuse.how: Reuse existing literal_f64(v) helper to construct CompiledExpr::literal(Value::Real(v)) for NaN and Inf distance values in the new Extrude tests
